key_insight: A multi-scale ML framework can jointly model chemical, spin, and structural disorder in alloys to predict temperature-driven phase behavior with experimental-level accuracy.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical computational tool for designing and screening complex magnetic alloys and other disordered materials relevant to high-entropy alloys, multiferroics, and spintronic devices.
method: Combines graph neural networks and machine-learning interatomic potentials to represent coupled disorder.
Uses Monte Carlo and molecular dynamics to sample thermodynamic and structural evolution across scales.
Applies the framework to bcc Fe-Co-C, capturing chemical, spin, and interstitial-induced lattice distortions.
evidence_signal: The framework predicts the Fe-Co order-to-disorder transition at 1,000 K and melting at 1,690 K, closely matching experiment (1,006 K and ~1,700 K).
watch_item: Accuracy depends on training data and model transferability, so performance may vary for alloys with different chemistry or disorder mechanisms.
paper_title: A Multi-Scale Machine Learning Framework for Coupled Chemical, Spin, and Structural Disorder in Alloys
